title: keckiella lemmonii
intro: Keckiella lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's Keckiella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Keckiella lemmonii include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Keckiella lemmonii can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems being steeped in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
